title: Web Hosting in Perth
meta_title: Web Hosting Perth | Managed & Unmanaged from $25/mo
meta_description: Web hosting Perth from $25/month, GST inclusive, no lock-in. Managed or unmanaged, explained honestly so you pick the right one. Australian owned, to 9 PM AWST.
intro: Two hosting plans, $25 and $50 a month with GST included and no lock-in, plus honest advice on which one you actually need. Australian owned and operated, working to AWST so we're on Perth time.
body_html:
What you're actually buying when you buy hosting

Hosting is the space your website lives in and the machine that serves it to visitors. Every host will tell you they're fast and reliable, so the useful questions are narrower: which plan covers what, who does the work of keeping the site up to date, and what it costs you every month with nothing hidden underneath.

OptiTide offers two hosting plans. Unmanaged hosting is $25 per month. Managed hosting is $50 per month. Both prices are in AUD and include GST, and there's no lock-in contract on either, so you're not signing a twelve-month term just to get the advertised rate.

Unmanaged vs managed: which one suits you

The difference between the two plans is not server quality. It's who does the work.

Unmanaged hosting ($25/month)

You get the hosting environment and you look after the site sitting on it. The updates, the keeping an eye on things and the working out why a page suddenly broke are yours. This suits people who are comfortable managing a site's updates themselves, businesses with someone technical in-house, or a simple brochure site that rarely changes.

It's the honest choice if you'd rather pay less and don't mind the occasional afternoon of troubleshooting. It's the wrong choice if nobody at your business actually wants that job, because in practice the updates then just don't get done, and outdated plugins and core files are a well-known way sites get compromised.

Managed hosting ($50/month)

Managed is the plan where the maintenance side is our job rather than yours. Instead of Googling error messages at nine o'clock at night, you email us. Exactly what's covered is set out on the hosting page, and it's worth reading that list before you choose, because the $25 gap between the two plans is entirely about how much of the work moves off your desk.

Managed is usually the right call if your website is doing real work for you, whether that's taking enquiries, taking bookings or selling something. On a site like that, an outage isn't only an inconvenience: it's enquiries you never hear about, because the person who couldn't load your page simply went and loaded somebody else's.

Why server location affects how fast your site feels

Distance is physics. Every request between a visitor's browser and the server has to cross the actual distance in between, and a site served from the other side of the world adds a delay to that round trip that no amount of clever caching fully removes. The delay is easiest to feel on the first load and on anything interactive, such as a contact form, a checkout or a booking step.

So it's worth asking any host you're weighing up, us included, which data centre region your plan actually runs in, and comparing that against where your customers are. A lot of very cheap plans are cheap for a reason, usually a distant server or a lot of sites packed onto one machine. Ask your current host which it is, and ask before you renew rather than after.

Speed matters for search as well as for patience. Slow pages lose people before they read anything, which is why page speed comes up in SEO work at all, and it's frustrating to optimise a site properly only for the server to hand the time straight back.

Moving an existing site across

If you're looking at web hosting Perth options because you already have a site somewhere and you're unhappy with it, whether it's slow, support takes days, or the renewal price jumped without warning, moving hosts is usually less painful than people expect.

Tell us where the site is hosted now and what it's built on, and we'll tell you what moving it would involve and what we can take off your hands. The one thing to sort out early is where your domain name is registered and who has access to that account, because that's the login that controls the switch. If you're not sure, say so and we'll help you work it out. It's an easy thing to have lost track of, particularly when somebody else set the site up years ago and has since moved on.

How we work with hosting clients

OptiTide is Michael James Long trading as OptiTide, ABN 38 163 865 712, Australian owned and operated, working remotely to AWST. We're not based in Perth, but we run on Perth time: our hours are Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM AWST, which is deliberately late into the evening. For anything to do with a website that matters more than it sounds: you can send something through after you've shut up shop for the day and look at the result the next morning.

You also get a portal, where you can view and pay invoices, track your project and message us directly, so hosting questions don't disappear into an inbox. Payment is by PayID or bank transfer, Skrill, PayPal or Payoneer. We work with clients right across Australia, remotely.

We also build websites, at $750 one-off for the Starter build, $1,500 one-off for Business, or a quoted price for a custom build, and hosting often comes up in that conversation. You don't need us to have built the site for us to host it, and you don't need to host with us to have us build one.

Getting started

If you know which plan you want, get in touch and we'll get you set up. If you're not sure whether unmanaged or managed makes sense, describe the site and who looks after it now. That's usually enough for us to give you a straight answer, including telling you when the cheaper $25 plan is genuinely all you need.

You can see the full plan details on the hosting page, read how we work before you commit to anything, or browse everything we do across our services.

faqs.q: How much does web hosting cost in Perth?
faqs.a: OptiTide has two plans: unmanaged hosting at $25 per month and managed hosting at $50 per month. Both prices are AUD and GST inclusive, with no lock-in contract. The difference between them is not the server, it's whether the ongoing maintenance is your job or ours. What each plan covers is listed on the hosting page.

faqs.q: What's the difference between managed and unmanaged hosting?
faqs.a: Unmanaged means you take the hosting environment and look after the site on it yourself. Managed means the maintenance side is our job rather than yours, so you contact us instead of working through it alone. Exactly what's covered under each plan is set out on the hosting page, so check it there before you choose. As a rule of thumb, unmanaged suits technically confident owners and simple sites that rarely change, while managed suits anyone whose website is actively bringing in enquiries or sales.

faqs.q: Does it matter where my website is hosted if my customers are in Australia?
faqs.a: Server distance affects speed. Every request has to travel the physical distance between your visitor and the server, so a site served from overseas adds delay to every page load, and that delay is easiest to feel on forms, checkouts and booking steps. Ask any host you're considering, us included, which data centre region your plan actually runs in, and compare that against where your customers are. Page speed also affects search performance, because slow pages lose visitors before they read anything.

faqs.q: Can you move my existing website to your hosting?
faqs.a: Tell us where the site is hosted now and what it's built on, and we'll tell you what moving it would involve and what we can take off your hands. The main thing to sort out early is who controls your domain name registration, since that's the account used to make the switch, so it's worth confirming you have access to it before anything else.

faqs.q: Do I have to have you build my website to host with you?
faqs.a: No. We're happy to host a site somebody else built. If you'd like the site rebuilt as well, our web design work starts at $750 one-off for the Starter tier, with Business at $1,500 one-off and custom builds quoted, but that's a separate decision from hosting.

faqs.q: What happens if my site goes down outside business hours?
faqs.a: Our hours are Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM AWST, which runs deliberately late into the evening, so if you notice something after you've closed for the day you're not automatically waiting until the next morning to reach us. Anything you raise through your client portal outside those hours is sitting there waiting for us when we're back.
